coherence_checks: Same-tag consistency: 'duty', 'prescribed-action', 'judgment-under-uncertainty' — these align with the karma-yoga block. The judgment-under-uncertainty tag is appropriate because the verse is precisely about identifying a class of moments under ambiguity (was this opportunity engineered or unsought?).

Cross-reference resolution: the engineering layer references Chapter 5 (right-renunciation) and 18.66 (closing release) for the 'opportunity born of colleague's burnout' case. Both are correct doctrinal pointers.

Cross-reference support: the citing claim (yadṛcchayā is necessary but not sufficient — opportunities born of injustice carry obligations of repair) is supported by Chapter 5's discussion of when withdrawal from action is warranted.
Consistency with 2.31: same role-bound framing (the operator's specific lane) carried across both verses. 2.31 names the lane; 2.32 names the moment in the lane.
verdict_reason: Doctrine coherent across tags and cross-references; consistent with neighbours. PASS on both 6.1 and 6.5.
